mechanism_narrative: Persistent low federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital and boosting corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat when FEDFUNDS is below 2% over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential recession, leading to a decline in consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with high inflation indicates robust consumer demand but also eroding purchasing power, potentially forcing the Fed to tighten, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as discount rates rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM AND CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% MoM
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive or flat when both RSAFS and CPIAUCSL show strong monthly growth for a sustained period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, the Federal Reserve's aggressive rate hikes (FEDFUNDS) increase the cost of capital and reduce liquidity, negatively impacting equity valuations and leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with high inflation
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive during periods of aggressive FEDFUNDS hikes when CPIAUCSL is above 5% year-over-year.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts indicates weakening consumer and business confidence, which typically precedes broader economic slowdowns and negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where HOUST declines by more than 10% over three months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust industrial production growth signals a healthy manufacturing sector and strong business investment, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO shows monthly growth above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong non-farm payrolls growth indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues, which generally leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 100K MoM for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods following three consecutive months of PAYEMS growth exceeding 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing GDP growth combined with rising federal funds rates indicates a stagflationary or recessionary environment where monetary policy is tightening into a weakening economy, leading to significant negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive or flat when GDPC1 growth is below 1% QoQ and FEDFUNDS is above 1.5%.
